page: Google Cloud Platform c2d-standard-4 is a general-purpose virtual machine instance featuring 4 vCPUs and 16 GB of system memory, translating to a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. Powered by an x86_64 AMD EPYC 7B13 processor, the instance allocates 2 dedicated physical cores with 2 threads per core. The CPU cache configuration includes 32 KB of L1d cache, 0.5 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache. This instance does not include bundled local storage or dedicated GPU hardware.

Benchmark data indicates a clear contrast between single-core and multi-core capabilities. The instance demonstrates strong single-core CPU performance in stress-ng testing, placing in the top 25%. However, multi-core performance is relatively weak, falling into the bottom 25% for stress-ng multi-core, Geekbench multi-core, and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression. Memory bandwidth for large uncached blocks and single-threaded compression tasks perform in the average tier, while small-block cached memory reads and database operations are weak.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the c2d-standard-4 offers a low-cost entry point for dedicated AMD EPYC resources, making it suitable for workloads that do not require high multi-threaded density or local NVMe storage. Given its strong single-core performance and average text processing capabilities, this instance is best suited for single-threaded utility scripts, lightweight background workers, small-scale text generation, and general-purpose development environments. It is less optimal for high-throughput web serving, database hosting, or parallelized compilation tasks due to its lower multi-core and database benchmark scores.
description: Google Cloud Platform c2d-standard-4 is a general-purpose virtual machine configured with 4 vCPUs and 16 GB of RAM, running on an x86_64 AMD EPYC 7B13 processor. The instance features 2 dedicated physical cores with hyper-threading, 32 MB of L3 cache, and lacks local storage or GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks reveal strong single-core CPU execution, ranking in the top 25%, while multi-core operations, database transactions, and static web serving throughput fall into the bottom 25%. Uncached memory bandwidth and single-threaded compression tasks perform in the average range. Given these characteristics, the c2d-standard-4 provides a cost-effective option for workloads prioritizing single-threaded execution over highly parallelized processing. It is best suited for lightweight background tasks, basic utility services, small-scale text processing, and general-purpose development environments rather than demanding database or high-throughput web hosting.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c2d-standard-4 is a general-purpose instance with 4 vCPUs and 16 GB RAM. It features an AMD EPYC 7B13 CPU, offering strong single-core performance but limited multi-core scaling.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c2d-standard-4 is an AMD EPYC virtual machine with 4 vCPUs and 16 GB RAM, offering strong single-core performance.
tagline: A budget-friendly virtual machine featuring dedicated AMD EPYC cores, delivering strong single-threaded performance for general-purpose utility workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 CPU with 4 vCPUs and 16 GB RAM
Strong single-core CPU performance ranking in the top 25%
Average memory bandwidth for large uncached blocks
No bundled local storage or GPU acceleration
Best suited for single-threaded tasks, utility scripts, and light development
